definition: Compute dynamic time warping distance between evaluation and training sequences and transform it to a bounded similarity. This axis is useful when the timing of a pattern can shift but the temporal shape remains scientifically meaningful.
example_usage: python time_series_dtw_similarity.py --train train_series.csv --eval eval_series.csv --out pairwise_similarity.csv --series-col series --scale 1.0

citations.title: WOODS: Benchmarks for Out-of-Distribution Generalization in Time Series

evidence.summary: WOODS identifies time-series OOD generalization as underexplored and reports large generalization gaps.
The benchmark describes source, time, device, demographic, and procedural distribution shifts.

citations.title: Similarity measure of time series based on Angle-distance Penalized Metric Dynamic Time Warping

evidence.summary: The paper frames DTW as a widely used time-series similarity method and defines its distance matrix and path.

limitations: DTW can align biologically or physically implausible time warps if unconstrained.
Amplitude normalization and sampling rate differences can dominate the distance.
For forecasting tasks, temporal order leakage must be prevented.
quality_gates: Report interpolation, normalization, sampling rate, and warping-window constraints.
Use a time-respecting train/evaluation split when forecasting future data.
Compare DTW similarity to simpler lag, season, or device/domain metadata axes.
